program: Travel English -- Europe Without a Translator
hub_subtitle: Travel English for the airport, the check-in counter, the hotel and passport control — so that Europe needs no translator

lesson.menu_title: Diagnostic -- Who Is Rosângela at the Airport Gate?
lesson.menu_desc: Introducing yourself to a traveler at the departure gate, and understanding the announcement that changes it all -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 1 -- Diagnostic at the Gate
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rosângela | Lesson 1 -- Diagnostic at the Gate
lesson.grammar_point: verb to be and present simple (I am / I live)

listenings.text: Good evening, ladies and gentlemen. This is an announcement for the passengers of flight 447 to Paris. We are sorry to tell you that your flight is delayed. The new departure time is nine o'clock, so there is a delay of two hours. We apologize for the delay and for the inconvenience. There is also a gate change. Flight 447 to Paris now leaves from gate B14, here in Terminal 3. Please go to gate B14 with your boarding pass and your passport. Boarding starts at eight thirty. Once again: flight 447 to Paris, new departure time nine o'clock, gate B14, Terminal 3. Thank you very much for your patience.

Hello. My name is Laura, and I am waiting for the Paris flight too. I am from Portugal. I live in Lisbon, near the river. I am 62 years old, and I am retired now. I travel once in a while, always with my sister. She is here too, at the coffee shop. This year we are going to Paris for one week. We love museums, and we walk everywhere, all day. My flight is at nine o'clock, from Terminal 3, and my gate is B14. The same as you, I think. My passport and my boarding pass are here, in my carry-on. Two hours of delay is a long time, but I am not in a hurry. So, tell me about you. Where are you from?

items.situation: "Hello! Is this seat free? I am Daniel." Say hello and tell him your name.
tips: Yes, please, sit down. I am Rosângela.

items.situation: "Where are you from?" Answer with a full sentence, not one word.
tips: I am from São Paulo, in Brazil.
I live in Vila Leopoldina, a neighborhood of São Paulo.

items.situation: "Where are you going today?" Tell him your destination and who is with you.
tips: I am going to Paris, with my husband.
My destination is Paris. It is a holiday.

items.situation: "Do you travel a lot?" Use the expression of the day.
tips: I travel to Europe once in a while.
Not every year. Once in a while, with my husband.

items.situation: "What time is your flight?" The screen says seven o'clock, but there is a delay of two hours.
tips: My flight is at seven o'clock, but there is a delay of two hours.
So now the departure is at nine o'clock.

items.situation: Now YOU ask HIM two questions. He is a stranger at the gate, and you have two hours.
tips: And you? Where are you from?
Where do you live? Do you travel a lot?
